title: 论文提炼｜A Framework of Knowledge Graph-Enhanced Large Language Model Based on Question Decomposition and Atomic Retrieval
content:
论文名称：A Framework of Knowledge Graph-Enhanced Large Language Model Based on Question Decomposition and Atomic Retrieval
研究主题：大模型推理、检索增强与记忆系统
发表 venue：EMNLP - Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing (Findings)
发表年份：2024
作者：Yading Li, Dandan Song, Changzhi Zhou, Yuhang Tian, Hao Wang, Ziyi Yang, Shuhao Zhang
作者角色：合作作者或共同作者
一句话总结：Knowledge graphs (KGs) can provide ex-plainable reasoning for large language models(LLMs), alleviating their hallucination problem.Knowledge graph question answering (KGQA)is a typical benchmark to evaluate the methodsenhancing LLMs with KG.
核心内容：
Knowledge graphs (KGs) can provide ex-plainable reasoning for large language models(LLMs), alleviating their hallucination problem.Knowledge graph question answering (KGQA)is a typical benchmark to evaluate the methodsenhancing LLMs with KG. Previous methodson KG-enhanced LLM for KGQA either en-hance LLMs with KG retrieval in a single roundor perform multi-hop KG reasoning in multi-ple rounds with LLMs. Both of them conductretrieving and reasoning based solely on thewhole original question, without any process-ing to the question. To tackle this limitation,we propose a framework of KG-enhanced LLMbased on question decomposition and atomic re-trieval, called KELDaR. We introduce questiondecomposition tree as the framework for LLMreasoning. This approach extracts the implicitinformation of reasoning steps within complexquestions, serving as a guide to facilitate atomicretrieval on KG targeting the atomic-level sim-ple questions at leaves of the tree. Additionally,we design strategies for atomic retrieval, whichextract and retrieve question-relevant KG sub-graphs to assist the few-shot LLM in answeringatomic-level questions. Experiments on KGQAdatasets demonstrate that our framework out-performs existing reasoning-based baselines.And in a low-cost setting without additionaltraining or fine-tuning, our framework achievescompetitive or superior results compared tomost existing training-based baselines.
适合回答的问题：
- 这篇论文主要解决什么问题，以及它为什么重要。
- 这篇论文的核心方法、系统设计或算法思路是什么。
- 这篇论文与 大模型推理、检索增强与记忆系统 这条研究主线的关系是什么。
